items.title: Run Clojure inside Go
items.oneliner: Early interpreter runs Clojure in Go apps with a REPL and direct access to Go libraries.

items.title: Study advanced compilers hands-on
items.oneliner: Self-guided Cornell course teaches LLVM, IR design, and optimization through real coding assignments and research readings.

items.title: Scale version control for binaries
items.oneliner: Open source VCS from Epic handles huge binary assets with cheap branching and content-addressed storage.

items.title: Emulate Windows and Linux userspace
items.oneliner: High-performance syscall-level emulator supports hooks, snapshots, Python scripting, and GDB for reverse engineering and security research.

items.title: Build agent memory on Elasticsearch
items.oneliner: Detailed architecture shows persistent agent memory with 0.89 recall using hybrid retrieval, rerankers, and audit-friendly storage.

items.title: Launch isolated browsers under one second
items.oneliner: Infra teardown explains Firecracker browser sessions on EC2 with sub-second startup and about $0.02 per browser hour.

items.title: Audit GitHub repos for malware
items.oneliner: Investigation includes a detection script for finding Trojan-distributing GitHub repos by commit and distribution patterns.

items.title: Use Git ignore beyond gitignore
items.oneliner: Practical Git guide shows repo-local and global ignore files, plus commands to debug why files are skipped.

items.title: Turn an old phone into a server
items.oneliner: Hands-on walkthrough repurposes a broken smartphone as a web server using hardware you already have.

items.title: Speed up Unity math calls
items.oneliner: Benchmark-backed analysis shows when System.MathF beats Mathf by avoiding hidden double-to-float conversion overhead.
